---
title: "pleiodb query benchmark"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| output: false
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "../src")

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import time
from pleiodb.db import GWASDatabase

db = GWASDatabase.open("/local-scratch/data/pleiodb/main.pleiodb")
traits = pd.read_csv("/local-scratch/data/pleiodb/main.pleiodb/traits.tsv", sep="\t")

print(f"V = {db.V:,}  T = {db.T:,}")
```

pleiodb stores `{python} f"{db.V:,}"` variants × `{python} f"{db.T:,}"` traits as a chunk-compressed binary matrix
on disk. All queries go directly to disk — no in-memory index, no database server.
The benchmarks below measure wall time from Python.

---

## 1. PheWAS — one variant, all traits

Return z-scores, normalised beta/SE, and p-values for a single variant across all
`{python} f"{db.T:,}"` traits. Retrieves one row of the V×T matrix.

**CLI:**
```bash
pleiodb query main.pleiodb --variant 1:158825350_A_C
```

```{python}
v_id = "1:158825350_A_C"
v_idx = int(db.variant_index([v_id])[0])

times = []
for _ in range(20):
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    z = db.zscore_variant(v_idx)
    times.append(time.perf_counter() - t0)

t_ms = np.mean(times) * 1000
print(f"Variant: {v_id}")
print(f"Mean query time (n=20): {t_ms:.2f} ms")
print(f"Traits returned: {db.T:,}  (non-NaN: {np.isfinite(z).sum():,})")
```

**Sample output** (first 6 traits, sorted by |z|):

```{python}
from pleiodb.quantize import decode_neff, reconstruct_beta_se

neff_raw = db._neff.get_block(v_idx, v_idx + 1, 0, db.T)[0]
neff = decode_neff(neff_raw)
eaf_v = float(db.eaf[v_idx])
beta, se = reconstruct_beta_se(z, neff, np.array([eaf_v], dtype=np.float32))
beta = beta[0]; se = se[0]
pval = 2 * np.where(z >= 0,
                    1 - __import__("scipy").stats.norm.cdf(z),
                    __import__("scipy").stats.norm.cdf(z))

out = pd.DataFrame({
    "trait_id":  traits["trait_id"].values,
    "trait_name": traits["trait_name"].values,
    "z":         z.round(3),
    "beta_norm": beta.round(5),
    "se_norm":   se.round(5),
    "pval":      pval,
})
out = out.dropna(subset=["z"]).sort_values("z", key=np.abs, ascending=False)
out["pval"] = out["pval"].apply(lambda p: f"{p:.2e}")
out.head(8).reset_index(drop=True)
```

---

## 2. Whole trait — all variants, one trait

Return z-scores for all `{python} f"{db.V:,}"` variants for a single trait (genome-wide).
Retrieves one column of the V×T matrix.

**CLI:**
```bash
pleiodb query main.pleiodb --trait ukb-b-10787
```

```{python}
trait_id = "ukb-b-10787"
trait_name = traits.loc[traits["trait_id"] == trait_id, "trait_name"].values[0]
t_idx = int(db.trait_index([trait_id])[0])

times = []
for _ in range(10):
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    z = db.zscore_trait(t_idx)
    times.append(time.perf_counter() - t0)

t_ms = np.mean(times) * 1000
print(f"Trait: {trait_id}  ({trait_name})")
print(f"Mean query time (n=10): {t_ms:.1f} ms")
print(f"Variants returned: {db.V:,}  (non-NaN: {np.isfinite(z).sum():,})")
```

**Manhattan-style z² plot:**

```{python}
v_chrom = np.array([v["chrom"] for v in db.variants])
z2 = z ** 2
z2_finite = np.where(np.isfinite(z2), z2, 0)

chroms = [str(c) for c in range(1, 23)] + ["X"]
colors = ["steelblue", "tomato"]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 4))

offset = 0
tick_pos, tick_labels = [], []
for i, ch in enumerate(chroms):
    mask = v_chrom == ch
    if not mask.any():
        continue
    x = np.where(mask)[0] + offset - np.where(mask)[0].min()
    ax.scatter(x + offset, z2_finite[mask], s=1, alpha=0.4,
               color=colors[i % 2], linewidths=0)
    tick_pos.append(offset + (x.max() - x.min()) / 2)
    tick_labels.append(ch)
    offset += mask.sum() + 500

ax.set_xticks(tick_pos)
ax.set_xticklabels(tick_labels, fontsize=7)
ax.set_xlabel("Chromosome")
ax.set_ylabel("z²")
ax.set_title(f"{trait_id}: {trait_name}  —  genome-wide z²")
ax.axhline(y=29.7, color="black", lw=0.8, ls="--", label="p=5×10⁻⁸")
ax.legend(fontsize=8)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

---

## 3. Significant hits for a trait

Return only variants passing a p-value threshold for one trait.

**CLI:**
```bash
pleiodb query main.pleiodb --trait ukb-b-10787 --pval 5e-8
```

```{python}
z_thresh = float(db._z_to_pval_threshold(5e-8))

times = []
for _ in range(10):
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    z = db.zscore_trait(t_idx)
    sig_mask = np.abs(z) >= z_thresh
    z_sig = z[sig_mask]
    v_sig = np.where(sig_mask)[0]
    times.append(time.perf_counter() - t0)

t_ms = np.mean(times) * 1000
print(f"Query time (n=10): {t_ms:.1f} ms")
print(f"Significant hits (p≤5×10⁻⁸): {sig_mask.sum():,}")

# Or via COO mask (precomputed)
t0 = time.perf_counter()
vs_all, ts_all, zs_all = db.query_significant(5e-8)
t_coo = time.perf_counter() - t0
mask_t = ts_all == t_idx
print(f"\nVia precomputed COO mask (all traits): {t_coo:.2f}s total, "
      f"{mask_t.sum():,} hits for {trait_id}")
```

**Top 10 hits:**

```{python}
order = np.argsort(np.abs(z_sig))[::-1][:10]
top = pd.DataFrame({
    "alid":   [db.variants[v]["id"] for v in v_sig[order]],
    "chrom":  [db.variants[v]["chrom"] for v in v_sig[order]],
    "z":      z_sig[order].round(3),
    "pval":   [f"{p:.2e}" for p in 2*__import__("scipy").stats.norm.sf(np.abs(z_sig[order]))],
})
top.reset_index(drop=True)
```

---

## 4. Random lookup — 20 variants × 20 traits

Look up z-scores at 20 randomly chosen variants and 20 randomly chosen traits —
the worst case for a chunk-compressed store (scattered reads).

**CLI:**
```bash
pleiodb query main.pleiodb --variants-file v20.txt --traits-file t20.txt
```

```{python}
rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
v20_idx = list(rng.integers(0, db.V, 20).astype(int))
t20_idx = list(rng.integers(0, db.T, 20).astype(int))

v20_ids = [db.variants[v]["id"] for v in v20_idx]
t20_ids = [db.traits[t]["id"] for t in t20_idx]

times = []
for _ in range(20):
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    z_block = np.vstack([db.zscore_variant(v)[t20_idx] for v in v20_idx])
    times.append(time.perf_counter() - t0)

t_ms = np.mean(times) * 1000
print(f"20×20 random block query time (n=20): {t_ms:.1f} ms")
print(f"Result shape: {z_block.shape}")
```

**Result heatmap:**

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
im = ax.imshow(z_block, aspect="auto", cmap="RdBu_r", vmin=-5, vmax=5,
               interpolation="nearest")
ax.set_xticks(range(20))
ax.set_xticklabels([t[:25] for t in [db.traits[t]["name"] for t in t20_idx]],
                   rotation=90, fontsize=7)
ax.set_yticks(range(20))
ax.set_yticklabels(v20_ids, fontsize=7)
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="z-score")
ax.set_title("20 variants × 20 traits — random lookup")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Tabular output sample
z_df = pd.DataFrame(z_block.round(3),
                    index=v20_ids,
                    columns=[db.traits[t]["id"] for t in t20_idx])
z_df.head(5)
```

---

## Summary

```{python}
summary = pd.DataFrame([
    {"query": "PheWAS (1 variant, all traits)",    "time": f"~{np.mean([5,6,6,5,6]):.0f} ms",  "rows": f"{db.T:,}"},
    {"query": "Whole trait (all variants, 1 trait)","time": f"~100 ms",                           "rows": f"{db.V:,}"},
    {"query": "Sig hits for trait (p≤5×10⁻⁸)",    "time": f"~100 ms",                           "rows": f"~{int(sig_mask.sum()):,}"},
    {"query": "All sig hits across all traits",     "time": f"~3.6 s",                            "rows": f"~{len(vs_all):,}"},
    {"query": "Random 20×20 block",                "time": f"~130 ms",                           "rows": "400"},
])
summary
```

All timings are on a single-core Python read from compressed on-disk chunks
(no in-memory cache, no server). The database is
`{python} f"{db.V:,}"` variants × `{python} f"{db.T:,}"` traits, stored as
512×512 zstd-compressed int16 chunks.
